howItWorks.intro: This student loan calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: What is the average student loan payment?
faq.answer: The average monthly student loan payment is about $350-400. The average borrower owes roughly $35,000 in student debt. Payments vary widely based on balance, rate, and repayment plan chosen.

faq.question: Should I choose standard or income-driven repayment?
faq.answer: Standard 10-year repayment minimizes total interest. Income-driven plans (IBR, PAYE, SAVE) cap payments at 5-15% of discretionary income but extend the term to 20-25 years, resulting in more total interest.

faq.question: Can I refinance student loans?
faq.answer: Private refinancing can lower your rate if you have good credit and stable income. However, refinancing federal loans into private loans forfeits federal benefits like income-driven repayment, forbearance, and loan forgiveness programs.
